display.purpose: Hero two-line headline only — upright first line, italic second line marking the emotional turn.

title.purpose: Section sub-headlines, cottage names, pull-quotes — set in italic by default.

body.purpose: Descriptive copy at reduced opacity (wet-slate/60), capped near 65–75ch.

label.purpose: Uppercase eyebrow tags and form-field captions, always at reduced opacity of the surrounding text color.

shadows.name: floating-panel
shadows.purpose: Nav 'Domki' dropdown — signals temporary layering above the page.

shadows.name: tilted-photograph
shadows.purpose: Rotated parallax portraits in the Memories section — makes a clipped div read as a physical print.

shadows.name: lifted-header
shadows.purpose: Scrolled nav bar once it detaches from the hero and gains a backdrop-blur surface.

motion.name: ease-reveal
motion.purpose: Default ease for scroll reveals, word-by-word hero entrance, and panel transitions — slow start, decisive settle.

motion.name: ease-button-mask
motion.purpose: Drives the signature circular hover-reveal mask on every button.

motion.name: duration-mask
motion.purpose: Length of the button hover-reveal clip-path animation.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Filled pill button with the system's signature circular hover-reveal mask. Used for every primary action (search, check availability, view cottage).
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { position: relative; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; background: #1C2320; color: #FDFAF5; font-family: 'Poppins', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; letter-spacing: 0.02em; padding: 12px 32px; border-radius: 9999px; border: none; overflow: hidden; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 0.1s; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.97); } .ds-btn-primary__reveal { position: absolute; inset: 0; background: #FDFAF5; clip-path: circle(0% at 50% 50%); transition: clip-path 0.5s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover .ds-btn-primary__reveal { clip-path: circle(150% at 50% 50%); } .ds-btn-primary__label { position: relative; z-index: 1; transition: color 0.15s 0.15s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover .ds-btn-primary__label { color: #1C2320; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #7A8E7E; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Outline pill button for use over photography (hero CTA, language switcher). Shares the identical reveal mask, inverted.
components.html: Poznaj domki ↓
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { position: relative; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; font-family: 'Poppins', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; padding: 8px 20px; border: 1px solid #FDFAF5; color: #FDFAF5; border-radius: 9999px; overflow: hidden; text-decoration: none; transition: transform 0.1s; } .ds-btn-ghost:active { transform: scale(0.97); } .ds-btn-ghost__reveal { position: absolute; inset: 0; background: #FDFAF5; clip-path: circle(0% at 50% 50%); transition: clip-path 0.5s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-ghost:hover .ds-btn-ghost__reveal { clip-path: circle(150% at 50% 50%); } .ds-btn-ghost__label { position: relative; z-index: 1; transition: color 0.15s 0.15s; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover .ds-btn-ghost__label { color: #1C2320; }

components.name: Underline Field
components.description: Borderless date/text field used throughout the booking widget and search form — a single hairline underline, no fill, no corner radius.
components.css: .ds-field__label { display: block; font-family: 'Poppins', sans-serif; font-size: 9px; letter-spacing: 0.2em; text-transform: uppercase; color: rgba(46,51,48,0.4); margin-bottom: 6px; } .ds-field__input { width: 100%; background: transparent; border: none; border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(46,51,48,0.15); color: #2E3330; font-family: 'Poppins', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; padding: 10px 0; transition: border-color 0.2s; } .ds-field__input:focus { outline: none; border-bottom-color: rgba(46,51,48,0.4); }

components.name: Photo Card
components.description: Image-led cottage/gallery card — large rounded corners, no border, no chrome. The photograph carries the card.
components.html:
70 m² · 6 os.

Domek nad Doliną Dunajca
components.css: .ds-card-photo { display: block; background: #FDFAF5; border-radius: 16px; overflow: hidden; text-decoration: none; transition: transform 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-card-photo:hover { transform: translateY(-4px); } .ds-card-photo__image { aspect-ratio: 4/3; background-size: cover; background-position: center; } .ds-card-photo__body { padding: 20px; } .ds-card-photo__eyebrow { font-family: 'Poppins', sans-serif; font-size: 10px; letter-spacing: 0.3em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #7A8E7E; margin: 0 0 8px; } .ds-card-photo__title { font-family: 'DM Serif Display', Georgia, serif; font-style: italic; font-size: 22px; line-height: 1.05; color: #2E3330; margin: 0; }

components.name: Editorial Panel
components.description: Square-cornered functional panel for the booking widget, search form, and result tiles — a deliberate counterpoint to the rounded photo cards: this surface is 'doing work', not 'showing the place'.
components.css: .ds-panel { background: #FDFAF5; border: 1px solid rgba(46,51,48,0.1); border-radius: 0; padding: 24px; font-family: 'Poppins', sans-serif; } .ds-panel__eyebrow { font-size: 10px; letter-spacing: 0.3em; text-transform: uppercase; color: rgba(46,51,48,0.4); margin: 0 0 20px; } .ds-panel__row { display: flex; justify-content: space-between; font-size: 14px; color: #2E3330; border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(46,51,48,0.15); padding: 10px 0; }

components.name: Nav Bar
components.description: Scrolled-state navigation bar — backdrop-blurred warm-white surface with a soft lifted-header shadow, replacing the transparent over-hero state.
components.html:
Malo Stay
DomkiGaleriaRezerwacja
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; padding: 16px 24px; background: rgba(253,250,245,0.95); backdrop-filter: blur(8px); box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(46,51,48,0.05); font-family: 'Poppins', sans-serif; } .ds-nav__logo { font-family: 'DM Serif Display', Georgia, serif; font-size: 18px; color: #2E3330; } .ds-nav__links { display: flex; gap: 32px; } .ds-nav__link { font-size: 14px; letter-spacing: 0.02em; color: #2E3330; text-decoration: none; transition: color 0.2s; } .ds-nav__link:hover { color: #2E7D52; }

narrative.overview:
Every screen is a frame onto the Dolina Dunajca, not a pitch for it. Panoramic hero photography, slow word-by-word reveals, and generous negative space all serve one idea: the visitor should feel like they are already looking out from the cottage, not scrolling through a brochure for it. Nothing here performs — type breathes at line-heights near 0.9, sections run for 30+ viewport-heights of quiet, and the one moment of color saturation (the deep greens) is rationed like a view held back until you've earned it.

The system rejects the entire vocabulary of Polish short-let marketing: no carousels, no "BOOK NOW" badges, no stacked trust-icons competing for attention. It also refuses the opposite trap — cold, sans-only, Airbnb-grey minimalism that reads as corporate rather than calm. Warmth comes from tinted neutrals (cream fog, bone white, slate charcoal — never true black or white), from a serif display face used in both upright and italic registers, and from photography that is allowed to simply be large. Mountain-resort ruggedness is also out of bounds: this is a valley, not an expedition — the mood is closer to a held breath than an adrenaline spike.
keyCharacteristics: Warm, green-tinted neutrals stand in for both "dark mode" and "light mode" — there is no true black or white anywhere in the system
Display serif (DM Serif Display) carries every emotional beat; italics mark the line where feeling overtakes information
Flat-by-default surfaces, defined by hairline borders rather than shadows
One micro-interaction — a circular bone-colored mask that blooms from a button's center on hover — repeats everywhere as the system's signature gesture
Sections alternate between cream (morning-fog), warm white (limestone-bone), and near-black green (dunajca-dusk) backgrounds to set a slow rhythm of light and shade down the page

rules.name: The No-True-Neutral Rule
rules.body: Every color in this system — including the ones that function as black, white, or grey — carries a green or cream tint (Dunajca Dusk instead of black, Limestone Bone instead of white, Wet Slate instead of charcoal-grey). If a value reads as a flat, untinted neutral, it does not belong here.

rules.name: The Sage Whisper Rule
rules.body: River Sage appears only in labels, italics, and small icon accents — never as a background fill or button color. Its rarity is what makes it register as a signal rather than decoration; the moment it fills a surface, it stops being a whisper.

rules.name: The Italic-Carries-Emotion Rule
rules.body: Upright display type states a fact; italic display type names a feeling. Every headline in the system follows this split deliberately — don't italicize for decoration, only at the exact word where the sentence turns from informational to felt.

rules.name: The Borders-First Rule
rules.body: Reach for a 1px hairline border before reaching for a shadow. Shadows are reserved for things that are actually floating — dropdowns, parallax images, a header that has detached from its hero — never for routine card or panel separation.

dos: Do keep every neutral tinted toward green or cream — Dunajca Dusk, Limestone Bone, Wet Slate, Morning Fog, Valley Mist. If a color could be mistaken for #000 or #fff, it's wrong for this system.
Do reserve River Sage for labels, italics, and small icon accents only — never as a fill.
Do mark the emotional turn in any headline with italic display type; keep the informational portion upright.
Do define surfaces with 1px hairline borders first; shadows are for things that are genuinely floating (dropdowns, parallax photos, the post-scroll header).
Do give every button the same circular bone-colored reveal on hover — it is the system's one repeated gesture, and repetition is what makes it feel intentional rather than accidental.
Do let sections run long and quiet — generous py-32/py-44 rhythm, short paragraphs capped near 65–75ch, large breathing room between ideas.
donts: Don't build anything that could be mistaken for a "generic Polish rental site template (OLX, nocleg.pl feel)" — no stock-photo grids competing for attention, no generic countryside-cottage clichés.

Don't reach for "WordPress holiday-park clones with carousels, badges, and 'BOOK NOW' everywhere" — no auto-rotating carousels, no urgency badges, no shouted CTAs. One calm, well-placed booking action per section is enough.

Don't drift toward "over-designed Airbnb-adjacent minimalism (cold white, sans-serif-only, zero warmth)" — if a screen reads as cold or corporate, the warmth (serif display, tinted cream neutrals, photography) has been lost somewhere.

Don't introduce "mountain resort branding (rugged, sporty, adventure-heavy)" — no carabiner iconography, no bold expedition typography, no high-energy action photography. This is a valley to rest in, not a peak to conquer.

Don't introduce one-off hex greens outside the token palette (e.g. an ad-hoc #2E7D52 action green sitting beside dunajca-dusk-filled buttons that do the same job) — consolidate every primary action to the same token so the green never reads as two different brands.

Don't add corner radius to the editorial/functional panels (search form, calendar, result tiles) — their square corners are what separates 'the system at work' from 'the system showing you a photograph.'
